Hello everybody, I am Dr. Cascio, your host on the first beefy video on your role as a practitioner and approaching client guidance with their long-term success in mind. For the practitioners specifically this transformation process which is essentially what it is is a two-headed monster. Okay you have side number one generating a plan for the client. This is generally considered programming and the second head of this monster number two is helping the client stick to that plan. This is effectively practicing or coaching as it were. This is exactly why this is separated into different components. There are two very different sets of skills. Programmers cannot always be practitioners but practitioners can always program. A crafty programmer, okay, programmers are okay, programmers are good, a crafty programmer will show and explain their methods to their clients. With their mind in the right place they can teach people the methods they use to program in hopes the client will apply it and the client is no longer with them. This is a reasonable way to expect long-term progress without pestering people. This is kind of like teaching a client to fish. You want to do this on your own, here's how I program good luck, right? A good practitioner, a coach, will work backward by assessing the client's behavior by assessing their feedback. The program is created around the client so adherence to the program is built in to the program creation process. This is a client-centric programming approach that promotes long-term success and permanent integration of these skills and behaviors into a person's lifestyle. This is effectively teaching a client to fish with dynamite, okay? Once behaviors are properly integrated into a person's lifestyle then decisions they make about health, fitness and wellness, they become intuitive, right? That means they're gut decisions are the right decisions and the client doesn't require as much babysitting. When integration of these behaviors and philosophies occurs the client is now able to incorporate more advanced skills which we know based off of the curriculum that we have those get incorporated way far in. So your first objective, your first objective is to provide people with the tools they require to be successful. Your second objective is to show people how to use these tools the way that is optimum for them. Your third objective is to have use of these tools be second nature to the client meaning they become intuitive. Like they now start to do behaviors on their own without having to think about them. That is called habit. So you're going to have clients habituate the skills that you are teaching them. And last but not least you want to provide these tools without being a tool yourself.
